_publications._publicationTitle: Medium throughput biochemical compound screening identifies novel agents for pharmacotherapy of neurofibromatosis type 1.
_publications._context: S462TY cells were generously provided by Dr. Timothy Cripe (Nationwide Children's Hospital, OH) | ...The human NF1 −/− MPNST cell line S462TY and NF1 + / human sporadic MPNST STS-26T cell line... | MPNST cell lines (STS-26T, S462, S462TY, T265) were obtained from Dr. David Largaespada's laboratory | S462-TY cells were cultured in DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin/streptomycin at 37°C with 5% CO2 | human MPNST cell lines (ST88-14, STS26T, S462, S462-TY, and T265) were cultured in complete media | The S462TY cell line was a kind gift from Dr. Timothy Cripe (Nationwide Children's Hospital Columbus, Columbus, OH, USA) | S462TY MPNST cells (1.8 × 10^6) from an NF1 patient were injected into four to five week old female nude mice | Other MPNST cell lines and patient-derived xenografts (PDX) were kindly provided by Dr Nancy Ratner (Cincinnati Children's Hospital) (26T, S462TY, ST88-14, MPNST724)

_publications._publicationTitle: Immune profiling of NF1-associated tumors reveals histologic subtype distinctions and heterogeneity: implications for immunotherapy.
Multiplatform molecular profiling uncovers two subgroups of malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors with distinct therapeutic vulnerabilities.
Pharmacogenomic Synthetic Lethal Screens Reveal Hidden Vulnerabilities and New Therapeutic Approaches for Treatment of NF1-Associated Tumors.
Forward genetic screen for malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor formation identifies new genes and pathways driving tumorigenesis.
FT895 Impairs Mitochondrial Function in Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor Cells.
Ras-driven transcriptome analysis identifies aurora kinase A as a potential malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor therapeutic target.
Multiomic analyses reveal new targets of polycomb repressor complex 2 in Schwann lineage cells and malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors.
